scenarios.title: LLM and Multimodal Training
scenarios.when_to_read: Use when a task asks to train, fine-tune, pre-train, LoRA/QLoRA tune, or debug ms-swift training commands.
scenarios.role: Provides ms-swift-specific routing and command/reference guidance for model training workflows.
scenarios.read_when: ms-swift, swift sft, swift pt, LoRA, QLoRA, ModelScope SWIFT, training config, adapters, checkpoints, multimodal training
scenarios.best_for: Building and validating `swift sft` or `swift pt` commands, training configs, checkpoint handoffs, and memory/debug choices.
scenarios.avoid_when: The task is about generic PyTorch training outside ms-swift or only about serving an already exported model.
scenarios.selection_guidance: Choose `ms-swift` over generic ML skills when the user names `swift`, `ms-swift`, or ModelScope SWIFT training commands or config files.

scenarios.title: Inference and Deployment
scenarios.when_to_read: Use when a task asks to infer, batch-generate, serve, deploy, or call ms-swift models through CLI, Python, or OpenAI-compatible APIs.
scenarios.role: Explains ms-swift inference/deployment routes, backend selection, adapters, multimodal inputs, and local serving checks.
scenarios.read_when: swift infer, swift app, swift deploy, vllm, sglang, lmdeploy, InferRequest, RequestConfig, adapters, served_model_name, OpenAI-compatible server
scenarios.best_for: Choosing `transformers` vs accelerated backends, building inference/deploy commands, smoke-testing local servers, and debugging multimodal serving.
scenarios.avoid_when: The task is only about training data schema or generic OpenAI API usage unrelated to ms-swift deployment.
scenarios.selection_guidance: Choose `ms-swift` when ms-swift is the serving framework or when adapter/checkpoint loading must follow SWIFT conventions.

scenarios.title: Data, Registry, and Template Customization
scenarios.when_to_read: Use when a task asks to prepare datasets, map columns, validate message rows, or register custom datasets/models/templates for ms-swift.
scenarios.role: Covers ms-swift data formats, preprocessors, external plugins, registries, and template/model selection.
scenarios.read_when: custom_dataset_info, external_plugins, register_dataset, register_model, register_template, messages format, rejected_response, loss_scale, images/videos/audios, tools
scenarios.best_for: Validating JSONL/CSV data, planning column mappings, authoring plugin skeletons, and checking registry/template behavior before training or inference.
scenarios.avoid_when: The task is only to run an already valid training command or evaluate a model without custom data/schema work.
scenarios.selection_guidance: Route to `ms-swift` first when ms-swift errors mention malformed rows, missing media, column mapping, model_type, template, or external plugin loading.

scenarios.title: Export, Quantization, Push, and Evaluation
scenarios.when_to_read: Use when a task asks to merge LoRA, export/quantize checkpoints, push artifacts, or evaluate with `swift eval` and EvalScope.
scenarios.role: Provides ms-swift-specific export, quantization, hub publishing, and evaluation guidance.
scenarios.read_when: swift export, swift merge-lora, swift eval, EvalScope, AWQ, GPTQ, FP8, BNB, push_to_hub, hub_model_id, eval_backend
scenarios.best_for: Choosing export/merge/quantization paths, checking custom eval datasets, and diagnosing missing EvalScope or quantization dependencies.
scenarios.avoid_when: The task only asks for live serving or generic benchmark design outside ms-swift/EvalScope.
scenarios.selection_guidance: Choose `ms-swift` when the requested output is a packaged model artifact, pushed hub repo, quantized checkpoint, cached dataset, or EvalScope result.

scenarios.title: RLHF, GRPO, Ray, and Megatron-SWIFT
scenarios.when_to_read: Use when a task asks for GRPO/RLHF/GKD/PPO/DPO/KTO/RM, reward plugins, sampling, rollout servers, Ray, or Megatron-SWIFT.
scenarios.role: Explains ms-swift advanced RL and distributed execution routes with optional dependency and parallelism caveats.
scenarios.read_when: swift rlhf, swift sample, swift rollout, megatron sft, megatron rlhf, GRPO, GKD, reward_func, ORM, PRM, Ray device_groups, TP, PP, CP, EP, Mcore-Bridge
scenarios.best_for: Planning reward/plugin signatures, rollout placement, Ray YAML, Megatron parallel dimensions, and optional backend checks.
scenarios.avoid_when: The task is ordinary SFT/pretraining without RL or Megatron/Ray-specific execution.
scenarios.selection_guidance: Choose `ms-swift` for ms-swift advanced RL/distributed workflows even when they also mention datasets or inference; use sibling sub-skills for shared schema or serving details.
